17	import os, re, subprocess, inspect, functools
18	import numpy as np
19	
20	# Fix some issues before importing other packages
21	from .import_fixes import fix_message_factory_issue
22	fix_message_factory_issue(); del fix_message_factory_issue;
23	
24	# Check if modules that need patching are already imported
25	critical_modules = ['trl', 'transformers', 'peft']
26	already_imported = [mod for mod in critical_modules if mod in sys.modules]
27	
28	# This check is critical because Unsloth optimizes these libraries by modifying
29	# their code at import time. If they're imported first, the original (slower,
30	# more memory-intensive) implementations will be used instead of Unsloth's
31	# optimized versions, potentially causing OOM errors or slower training.
32	
33	if already_imported:
34	    # stacklevel=2 makes warning point to user's import line rather than this library code,
35	    # showing them exactly where to fix the import order in their script
36	    warnings.warn(
37	        f"WARNING: Unsloth should be imported before {', '.join(already_imported)} "
38	        f"to ensure all optimizations are applied. Your code may run slower or encounter "
39	        f"memory issues without these optimizations.\n\n"
40	        f"Please restructure your imports with 'import unsloth' at the top of your file.",
41	        stacklevel = 2,
42	    )
43	pass
44	
45	# Unsloth currently does not work on multi GPU setups - sadly we are a 2 brother team so
46	# enabling it will require much more work, so we have to prioritize. Please understand!
47	# We do have a beta version, which you can contact us about!
48	# Thank you for your understanding and we appreciate it immensely!
49	
50	# Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/1266
51	os.environ["PROTOCOL_BUFFERS_PYTHON_IMPLEMENTATION"] = "python"
52	
53	# [TODO] Check why some GPUs don't work

55	#    "pinned_num_register_threads:8"
56	
57	# Log Unsloth is being used

59

72	@functools.cache
73	def is_hip():
74	    return bool(getattr(getattr(torch, "version", None), "hip", None))
75	pass

77	@functools.cache
78	def get_device_type():
79	    if hasattr(torch, "cuda") and torch.cuda.is_available():
80	        if is_hip():
81	            return "hip"
82	        return "cuda"
83	    elif hasattr(torch, "xpu") and torch.xpu.is_available():

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass
174	pass

177	from .swiglu import swiglu_fg_kernel, swiglu_DWf_DW_dfg_kernel
178	def apply_lora_mlp_swiglu(self, X, inplace = True):
179	    X = _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X, self.gate_proj)
180	    gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS = get_lora_parameters(self.gate_proj)
181	    upW,     upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS = get_lora_parameters(self.  up_proj)
182	    downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS = get_lora_parameters(self.down_proj)
183	    out = LoRA_MLP.apply(X,
184	                         gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
185	                         upW,     upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
186	                         downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
187	                         swiglu_fg_kernel, swiglu_DWf_DW_dfg_kernel,
188	                         inplace,)
189	    return out
190	pass

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass
276	
277	    @staticmethod
278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass
156	pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

463	        new_save_directory = save_directory
464	        username = new_save_directory[:new_save_directory.find("/")]
465	        new_save_directory = new_save_directory[new_save_directory.find("/")+1:]
466	        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
467	            new_save_directory = os.path.join(KAGGLE_TMP, new_save_directory[new_save_directory.find("/")+1:])
468	            logger.warning_once(

483	    print("Unsloth: Merging 4bit and LoRA weights to 16bit...")
484	
485	    # Determine max RAM usage minus sharding

488	    if type(max_shard_size) is str:
489	        gb_found = re.match(r"([0-9]{1,})[\s]{0,}GB", max_shard_size, flags = re.IGNORECASE)
490	        mb_found = re.match(r"([0-9]{1,})[\s]{0,}MB", max_shard_size, flags = re.IGNORECASE)
491	        if   gb_found: sharded_ram_usage = int(gb_found.group(1)) * 1024 * 1024 * 1024
492	        elif mb_found: sharded_ram_usage = int(mb_found.group(1)) * 1024 * 1024
493	    elif type(max_shard_size) is int:
494	        sharded_ram_usage = sharded_ram_usage
495	    pass
496	
497	    # Switch to our fast saving modules if it's a slow PC!
498	    n_cpus = psutil.cpu_count(logical = False)
499	    if n_cpus is None: n_cpus = psutil.cpu_count()

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass

261	    """
262	    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
263	    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )

234	
235	def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
236	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
237	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
238	    W = base_layer.weight
239	
240	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
241	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
242	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
243	    pass
244	
245	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
246	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
247	    adapter = adapter[0]
248	
249	    return (
250	        W,
251	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
252	        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
253	        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
254	        proj.scaling[adapter],
255	        base_layer.bias,
256	    )
257	pass

260	def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
261	    """
262	    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
263	    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
264	    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
265	    """
266	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
267	    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
268	    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
269	        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
270	    return X
271	pass

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass

381	class LoRA_W(torch.autograd.Function):
382	    """
383	    ### LoRA weights
384	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
385	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
386	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
387	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
388	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
389	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
390	
391	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
392	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
393	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
394	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
395	
396	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
397	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
398	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
399	
400	    ### K projection LoRA weights
401	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
402	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
403	
404	    ### V projection LoRA weights
405	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
406	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
407	    """
408	    @staticmethod
409	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
410	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
411	                W, W_quant, A, B, S):
412	        dtype = X.dtype
413	        XW = matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, S)
414	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (W, W_quant, S,)
415	        ctx.save_for_backward(A, B, X)
416	        return XW
417	    pass
418	
419	    @staticmethod
420	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
421	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
422	        W, W_quant, S = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
423	        A, B, X = ctx.saved_tensors
424	
425	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
426	        dY = dY.reshape(-1, dY.shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
427	        X  = X .reshape(-1, X .shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
428	        dtype = X.dtype
429	
430	        A, B = A.to(dtype), B.to(dtype)
431	
432	        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
433	
434	        d_A = torch.empty_like(A)
435	        d_B = torch.empty_like(B)
436	
437	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
438	        # Weight projection
439	        # d_A = X.t() @ (dY @ B.t())
440	        # d_B = (A.t() @ X.t()) @ dY
441	        # d_A *= S
442	        # d_B *= S
443	        d_A.addmm_(X.t(), dY @ B.t(), alpha = S, beta = 0)
444	        d_B.addmm_(A.t() @ X.t(), dY, alpha = S, beta = 0)
445	
446	        # Get derivative for dX
447	        W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant)
448	        dX = dY @ W.t()
449	        del W
450	        # dX += dY @ B.to(dtype).t() @ (S * A.to(dtype).t())
451	        dX.addmm_(dY @ B.t(), A.t(), alpha = S)
452	
453	        # W, W_quant, A, B, S
454	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
455	            None, None, d_A.t(), d_B.t(), None
456	    pass
457	pass

463	    O = LoRA_W.apply(X, OW, OW_quant, OA, OB, OS)
464	    return O

    A = lora_A_linear.weight

        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
    )

def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
pass


def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
    """
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive summary document.

## Unsloth LoRA Storage and Management: Complete Analysis

Based on my exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's a comprehensive guide to how LoRA adapter weights are stored and managed:

---

### 1. **Main Model Classes**

**Location:** `/unsloth/models/loader.py` and `/unsloth/models/llama.py`

- **`FastLanguageModel`** - Main class for language model LoRA training
- **`FastVisionModel`** - For vision models with LoRA
- **`FastTextModel`** - For text models
- **`FastModel`** - Base class for all models

All inherit from `FastLlamaModel` which handles LoRA configuration and patching.

---

### 2. **LoRA A and B Matrix Storage**

**How they're stored in PEFT layers:**

The LoRA weights are managed by PEFT (Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning) library and accessed through **`proj.lora_A` and `proj.lora_B`** dictionaries.

**Key Function:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` - `get_lora_parameters(proj)`

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    """
    # Get base layer (handles both standalone and wrapped layers)
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight  # Base weight matrix
    
    # Skip if adapters are disabled or merged
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    
    # Get active adapter name (typically "default")
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default",))
    adapter = adapter[0]  # Get first adapter name
    
    # Access LoRA weight matrices
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]    # nn.Linear module
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]    # nn.Linear module
    A = lora_A_linear.weight                # Shape: (r, in_features)
    B = lora_B_linear.weight                # Shape: (out_features, r)
    
    # Return all components
    return (W, quant_state, A, B, proj.scaling[adapter])
```

**Also available:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` - `get_lora_parameters_bias(proj)`
- Same as above but includes the base layer's bias parameter

---

### 3. **Rank (r), Alpha (lora_alpha), and Target Modules Storage**

These are stored in **`model.peft_config`** dictionary:

```python
# Access the LoRA configuration
peft_config = model.peft_config["default"]  # or any adapter name

# Get rank parameter
rank = peft_config.r  # or peft_config["r"]

# Get alpha parameter  
lora_alpha = peft_config.lora_alpha  # or peft_config["lora_alpha"]

# Get target modules
target_modules = peft_config.target_modules  # List of module names

# Other config parameters accessible:
peft_config.lora_dropout
peft_config.bias
peft_config.init_lora_weights
peft_config.task_type
peft_config.modules_to_save

# Convert to dictionary
config_dict = peft_config.to_dict()
```

**Location where LoraConfig is created:** `/unsloth/models/llama.py`, lines 2598-2617

The config is created with:
```python
lora_config = LoraConfig(
    r = r,
    lora_alpha = lora_alpha,
    target_modules = final_modules,
    lora_dropout = lora_dropout,
    bias = bias,
    task_type = TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,
    layers_to_transform = layers_to_transform,
    init_lora_weights = init_lora_weights,
    loftq_config = loftq_config,
    use_rslora = use_rslora,
    modules_to_save = modules_to_save,
)
```

---

### 4. **How to Iterate Over LoRA Layers to Get A and B Weights**

**Method 1: Iterate via named_parameters()**

```python
# Find all LoRA weight tensors
is_lora_weight = lambda name: any(s in name for s in ["lora_A", "lora_B"])
for name, param in model.named_parameters():
    if is_lora_weight(name):
        print(f"{name}: shape={param.shape}")
        # Example names:
        # model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj.lora_A.default.weight
        # model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj.lora_B.default.weight
```

**Method 2: Access via layer modules directly**

```python
from unsloth.kernels import get_lora_parameters, get_lora_parameters_bias

# For any projection layer in the model
for name, module in model.named_modules():
    # Check if it's a LoRA-enabled layer
    if hasattr(module, 'lora_A') and hasattr(module, 'lora_B'):
        # Get LoRA parameters
        W, quant_state, A, B, scaling = get_lora_parameters(module)
        
        if A is not None:  # Has LoRA adapters
            print(f"{name}:")
            print(f"  A shape: {A.shape}, B shape: {B.shape}")
            print(f"  Scaling factor: {scaling}")
            print(f"  Rank (A cols = B rows): {A.shape[1]}")
```

**Method 3: Access specific layer types**

Unsloth optimizes these specific projection layers:
```python
target_modules = [
    "q_proj",      # Query projection in attention
    "k_proj",      # Key projection in attention
    "v_proj",      # Value projection in attention
    "o_proj",      # Output projection in attention
    "gate_proj",   # Gate in MLP (for SwiGLU/GeGLU)
    "up_proj",     # Up projection in MLP
    "down_proj",   # Down projection in MLP
]

# Access each
for layer_idx in range(len(model.model.layers)):
    layer = model.model.layers[layer_idx]
    
    # Attention LoRA weights
    q_proj = layer.self_attn.q_proj
    W_q, _, A_q, B_q, scale_q = get_lora_parameters(q_proj)
    
    # MLP LoRA weights
    gate_proj = layer.mlp.gate_proj
    up_proj = layer.mlp.up_proj
    down_proj = layer.mlp.down_proj
```

---

### 5. **PEFT Integration - How Unsloth Wraps PEFT's LoRA**

**Location:** `/unsloth/models/llama.py` - `get_peft_model()` method (lines 2266-2815)

Unsloth's integration with PEFT:

1. **Imports PEFT components:**
   ```python
   from peft import LoraConfig, TaskType, get_peft_model as _get_peft_model
   from peft.tuners.lora import Linear, Linear4bit  # PEFT LoRA layer types
   ```

2. **Creates LoraConfig from parameters:**
   ```python
   lora_config = LoraConfig(
       r = rank,
       lora_alpha = lora_alpha,
       target_modules = target_modules,
       # ... other params
   )
   ```

3. **Applies PEFT to model:**
   ```python
   model = _get_peft_model(model, lora_config)
   ```

4. **Then patches forward passes for optimization:**
   ```python
   # From mistral.py, llama.py, etc.
   PeftModelForCausalLM.forward = PeftModel_fast_forward
   ```

**Key wrapper function:** `/unsloth/models/llama.py` - `PeftModel_fast_forward()` (lines ~2160-2265)
- Optimizes PEFT's forward pass
- Uses Unsloth's fast LoRA kernels
- Patches the base forward to use optimized computation

**Attribute structure for a LoRA layer:**
```python
# A PEFT LoRA Linear layer has:
proj.base_layer          # Original weight matrix
proj.lora_A              # Dict[adapter_name -> nn.Linear]
proj.lora_B              # Dict[adapter_name -> nn.Linear]
proj.scaling             # Dict[adapter_name -> scaling_factor]
proj.active_adapters     # Tuple of active adapter names, e.g., ("default",)
proj.merged              # bool - whether LoRA is merged into base
proj.disable_adapters    # bool - whether adapters are disabled
proj.weight              # Computed weight = base + scaling * (A @ B)
```

---

### 6. **LoRA Weight Computation Formula**

From the code comments in `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`:

```
# Forward pass
Modified_Weight = Base_Weight + scaling_factor * (A @ B)

# For projections:
output = input @ Modified_Weight

# Example for Q projection:
Q = X @ (Wq + s * Aq @ Bq)
  = X @ Wq + s * (X @ Aq @ Bq)
```

Where:
- `A` has shape `(r, in_features)` 
- `B` has shape `(out_features, r)`
- `s` (scaling) = `lora_alpha / r` (typically)
- `r` is the rank parameter

---

### 7. **Saving LoRA Weights**

**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py` - `_merge_lora()` function (lines 159-187)

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    """Merge LoRA adapters into base weights"""
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
    
    if A is not None:  # Has LoRA adapters
        # Merge: W = W + s * (A.T @ B.T)
        W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
    
    return W, bias
```

During model saving:
1. Unsloth can save only LoRA weights (via PEFT's native save)
2. Or merge LoRA into base weights and save merged model
3. Or use `unsloth_save_model()` for optimized saving

---

### 8. **No Existing ONNX Support**

**Finding:** There is **NO ONNX-related code** currently in the unsloth codebase.

```bash
grep -r "onnx\|ONNX" /unsloth/ --include="*.py"  # Returns: (no output)
```

This means ONNX export for LoRA models would need to be implemented from scratch or integrated separately.

---

### 9. **Key Classes and Their Locations**

| Class | Location | Purpose |
|-------|----------|---------|
| `FastLanguageModel` | `models/loader.py` | Main API for language models |
| `FastVisionModel` | `models/loader.py` | Vision model support |
| `FastLlamaModel` | `models/llama.py` | Base implementation for Llama-like models |
| `LoRA_QKV` | `kernels/fast_lora.py` | Custom autograd function for Q,K,V projections |
| `LoRA_MLP` | `kernels/fast_lora.py` | Custom autograd function for MLP layers |
| `LoRA_W` | `kernels/fast_lora.py` | Generic LoRA weight application |

---

### 10. **Helper Functions Reference**

| Function | Location | Purpose |
|----------|----------|---------|
| `get_lora_parameters()` | `kernels/utils.py:190` | Extract LoRA A, B, scaling from a layer |
| `get_lora_parameters_bias()` | `kernels/utils.py:235` | Same as above + includes bias |
| `apply_lora_qkv()` | `kernels/fast_lora.py:366` | Apply LoRA to Q,K,V projections |
| `apply_lora_o()` | `kernels/fast_lora.py:460` | Apply LoRA to output projection |
| `apply_lora_mlp_swiglu()` | `kernels/fast_lora.py:178` | Apply LoRA to SwiGLU MLP |
| `_merge_lora()` | `save.py:159` | Merge LoRA into base weights |
| `fast_dequantize()` | `kernels/utils.py:277+` | Dequantize 4-bit weights when needed |

---

### 11. **Accessing Configuration After Model Loading**

```python
from unsloth import FastLanguageModel

model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(...)
model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(model, r=16, lora_alpha=16, ...)

# Access configuration
config = model.peft_config["default"]
print(f"Rank: {config.r}")
print(f"Alpha: {config.lora_alpha}")
print(f"Target modules: {config.target_modules}")
print(f"Dropout: {config.lora_dropout}")

# Access actual weight tensors
for name, param in model.named_parameters():
    if "lora_" in name:
        print(f"{name}: {param.shape}")

# Get specific layer
q_proj_layer = model.model.layers[0].self_attn.q_proj
if hasattr(q_proj_layer, 'lora_A'):
    adapter_name = q_proj_layer.active_adapters[0]
    lora_a_weight = q_proj_layer.lora_A[adapter_name].weight
    lora_b_weight = q_proj_layer.lora_B[adapter_name].weight
    scaling = q_proj_layer.scaling[adapter_name]
```

---

This comprehensive analysis covers all the key aspects of how unsloth stores and manages LoRA adapter weights, providing exact class names, method signatures, and attribute names needed for integration or extension work like ONNX export.

### 3. LoRA-RELATED CODE ORGANIZATION

**Primary file:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (LoRA parameter extraction)
**Secondary file:** `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` (LoRA forward/backward)
**Integration:** `/unsloth/models/_utils.py` (LoRA patching into PEFT)

#### LoRA Adapter Structure (PEFT-based):

Each LoRA-enabled layer has:

```python
layer.lora_A[adapter_name]      # Linear layer with LoRA A matrix
layer.lora_B[adapter_name]      # Linear layer with LoRA B matrix  
layer.scaling[adapter_name]     # Scaling factor (lora_alpha)
layer.base_layer                # Original layer (for 4-bit: Bnb_Linear4bit or Peft_Linear4bit)
layer.merged                     # Boolean: whether LoRA is merged
layer.disable_adapters          # Boolean: adapter status
layer.active_adapters           # List of active adapter names (usually ["default"])
```

#### LoRA Parameter Extraction Functions:

**`get_lora_parameters(proj)` → (W, quant_state, A, B, scaling)**

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """Return (weight, weight_quant_state, lora_A, lora_B, lora_scale)"""
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default",))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Extract actual tensors
    A = proj.lora_A[adapter].weight      # Shape: (rank, input_dim)
    B = proj.lora_B[adapter].weight      # Shape: (output_dim, rank)
    s = proj.scaling[adapter]            # Scalar: lora_alpha / rank
    
    return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), A, B, s
```

**`get_lora_parameters_bias(proj)` → (W, quant_state, A, B, scaling, bias)**

Returns same as above plus `base_layer.bias`

#### LoRA Matrices Details:

- **A matrix**: `proj.lora_A[adapter_name].weight` - Shape: `(rank, in_features)`
- **B matrix**: `proj.lora_B[adapter_name].weight` - Shape: `(out_features, rank)`
- **Scaling**: `proj.scaling[adapter_name]` - Computed as `lora_alpha / r` in PEFT
- **Computation**: Output = Base + (A.T @ B.T) * scaling

#### LoRA Merging Process (in `_merge_lora`):

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
    
    # Dequantize if 4-bit
    if quant_state is not None:
        W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
    
    W = W.to(torch.float32).t()  # Transpose for matmul
    
    if A is not None:  # Merge: W += A.T @ B.T * s
        W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
    
    W = W.t().to(dtype)  # Transpose back
    return W, bias
```

**Layers processed during merge:**
```python
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
    "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj",
)
```

#### Fast LoRA Custom Autograd (in `fast_lora.py`):

**`LoRA_MLP` class**: Custom PyTorch autograd function for efficient MLP with LoRA
- Saves A, B matrices for backward pass
- Computes gradients efficiently using saved tensors
- Returns tuple of gradients for all LoRA parameters

**Helper functions:**
- `apply_lora_mlp_swiglu()` - Apply LoRA to SwiGLU MLP
- `apply_lora_mlp_geglu_exact()` - Apply LoRA to GeGLU MLP
- Uses `swiglu_fg_kernel`, `swiglu_DWf_DW_dfg_kernel` for fused kernels

```python
        "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"
    ],

    "use_rslora": False,                  # Rank-stabilized LoRA

    "layers_to_transform": None,          # Which layers to apply LoRA to

}
```

```python
    bias="none",

```


---

### 6. HOW SAVE METHODS ARE STRUCTURED

**Merge and Save Flow:**

```
Model with LoRA
    ↓
For each layer in model.layers:
    ├─ Extract: (W, A, B, scaling)
    ├─ Dequantize W if 4-bit
    ├─ Merge: W += A.T @ B.T * scaling
    └─ Save merged weights
    ↓
Save to disk/hub in chosen format
```

**Key layer weights merged:**
- Attention: q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj
- MLP: gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj
- LayerNorms: input_layernorm, post_attention_layernorm (NOT merged, just saved)

**Save directory structure for merged models:**
```
save_dir/
├── config.json                  # Model config (no quantization_config)
├── model.safetensors            # Merged weights in safetensors
├── tokenizer.model              # Tokenizer
├── tokenizer_config.json        # Tokenizer config
└── special_tokens_map.json      # Special tokens
```

**Save directory structure for LoRA-only:**
```
save_dir/
├── adapter_config.json          # LoRA config (r, alpha, target_modules)
├── adapter_model.safetensors    # LoRA A & B matrices
├── tokenizer.*                  # Tokenizer files
└── config.json                  # Model config
```

---

### 7. KEY FILES & THEIR PURPOSES

| File Path | Lines | Purpose |
|-----------|-------|---------|
| `unsloth/save.py` | 2,678 | Core save/export functionality; handles merging, GGUF, GGML |
| `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | ~700 | LoRA parameter extraction; quantization dequantize |
| `unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` | ~400 | Custom autograd for efficient LoRA forward/backward |
| `unsloth/models/llama.py` | ~2,000 | Llama model patching & LoRA integration |
| `unsloth/models/_utils.py` | ~1,800 | Shared utilities; LoRA patching into PEFT |
| `unsloth/models/loader.py` | ~1,000 | FastLanguageModel & FastModel loaders |
| `unsloth/__init__.py` | 270 | Package initialization; version management |
| `unsloth/models/__init__.py` | 29 | Model class exports |

---

### 8. CRITICAL INSIGHTS FOR LoRA ADAPTER WEIGHTS

**Storage pattern:**
- LoRA adapters stored in PEFT layers with dictionaries keyed by adapter name
- Default adapter: `"default"` key
- Multiple adapters supported simultaneously

**Access pattern to save LoRA weights:**
```python
for name, module in model.named_modules():
    if hasattr(module, 'lora_A'):  # Has LoRA
        for adapter_name, lora_a in module.lora_A.items():
            A = lora_a.weight  # Shape: (rank, in_features)
            B = module.lora_B[adapter_name].weight  # Shape: (out_features, rank)
            s = module.scaling[adapter_name]  # Float: scaling factor
            # ... save A, B, s ...
```

**Quantization state (4-bit models):**
- Stored in base weight: `W.quant_state` (from bitsandbytes)
- Needed to properly dequantize before merging
- Handled by `fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)` in kernels/utils.py

---

This completes the comprehensive exploration. The codebase is well-organized with clear separation between model loading (models/), optimization kernels (kernels/), and save/export functionality (save.py).

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """Return (weight, weight_quant_state, lora_A, lora_B, lora_scale)"""
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default",))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Extract actual tensors
    A = proj.lora_A[adapter].weight      # Shape: (rank, input_dim)
    B = proj.lora_B[adapter].weight      # Shape: (output_dim, rank)
    s = proj.scaling[adapter]            # Scalar: lora_alpha / rank
    
    return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), A, B, s
```

**`get_lora_parameters_bias(proj)` → (W, quant_state, A, B, scaling, bias)**

Returns same as above plus `base_layer.bias`

#### LoRA Matrices Details:

- **A matrix**: `proj.lora_A[adapter_name].weight` - Shape: `(rank, in_features)`
- **B matrix**: `proj.lora_B[adapter_name].weight` - Shape: `(out_features, rank)`
- **Scaling**: `proj.scaling[adapter_name]` - Computed as `lora_alpha / r` in PEFT
- **Computation**: Output = Base + (A.T @ B.T) * scaling

#### LoRA Merging Process (in `_merge_lora`):

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
    
    # Dequantize if 4-bit
    if quant_state is not None:
        W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
    
    W = W.to(torch.float32).t()  # Transpose for matmul
    
    if A is not None:  # Merge: W += A.T @ B.T * s
        W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
    
    W = W.t().to(dtype)  # Transpose back
    return W, bias
```

**Layers processed during merge:**
```python
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
    "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj",
)
```

#### Fast LoRA Custom Autograd (in `fast_lora.py`):

**`LoRA_MLP` class**: Custom PyTorch autograd function for efficient MLP with LoRA
- Saves A, B matrices for backward pass
- Computes gradients efficiently using saved tensors
- Returns tuple of gradients for all LoRA parameters

**Helper functions:**
- `apply_lora_mlp_swiglu()` - Apply LoRA to SwiGLU MLP
- `apply_lora_mlp_geglu_exact()` - Apply LoRA to GeGLU MLP
- Uses `swiglu_fg_kernel`, `swiglu_DWf_DW_dfg_kernel` for fused kernels

---

### 4. LoRA METADATA & CONFIGURATION

**PEFT Config Access:**

```python
model.peft_config["default"].to_dict()  # Get LoRA config dict
```

**LoRA Config Parameters:**

```python
peft_config = {
    "r": 16,                              # LoRA rank
    "lora_alpha": 16,                     # LoRA alpha (used in scaling)
    "lora_dropout": 0.0,                  # Dropout on LoRA inputs
    "bias": "none",                       # Bias handling: "none", "all", "lora_only"
    "target_modules": [                   # Which modules to apply LoRA to
        "q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
        "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"
    ],
    "modules_to_save": ["lm_head"],       # Full modules to save alongside LoRA
    "use_rslora": False,                  # Rank-stabilized LoRA
    "init_lora_weights": True,            # Initialize LoRA weights
    "loftq_config": None,                 # LoftQ quantization config
    "layers_to_transform": None,          # Which layers to apply LoRA to
    "layers_pattern": None,               # Layer naming pattern
    "base_model_name_or_path": "...",     # Source model
}
```

**Accessing config during get_peft_model:**
```python
model = FastLlamaModel.get_peft_model(
    model,
    r=16,
    target_modules=["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"],
    lora_alpha=16,
    lora_dropout=0.0,
    bias="none",
    layers_to_transform=None,
    layers_pattern=None,
    use_gradient_checkpointing="unsloth",
    use_rslora=False,
    modules_to_save=None,
)
```

---

### 5. PUBLIC API EXPOSURE

**Main `__init__.py` exports:**

```python
# From /unsloth/__init__.py
from .models import *                    # Model loaders & classes
from .models import __version__          # Version info
from .save import *                      # Save functions
from .chat_templates import *            # Chat template utilities
from .tokenizer_utils import *           # Tokenizer utilities
from .trainer import *                   # Trainer patches

# Key classes exposed:
FastLanguageModel                        # Main loader for language models
FastVisionModel                          # Vision model loader
FastTextModel, FastModel                 # Generic model loaders
PatchDPOTrainer, PatchKTOTrainer        # DPO/KTO trainer patches
```

**Models `__init__.py` exports:**

```python
# From /unsloth/models/__init__.py
FastLlamaModel           # Llama model handler
FastLanguageModel        # Extends FastLlamaModel
FastVisionModel          # Vision models
FastTextModel, FastModel # Generic handlers
FastMistralModel         # Mistral
FastQwen2Model           # Qwen2
FastQwen3Model           # Qwen3
FastQwen3MoeModel        # Qwen3 MoE
FastGraniteModel         # Granite
FastFalconH1Model        # Falcon (conditional)
PatchDPOTrainer          # DPO trainer patch
PatchKTOTrainer          # KTO trainer patch
PatchFastRL              # RL patches
is_bfloat16_supported()  # Device capability check
is_vLLM_available()      # vLLM availability
```

**Save module exports:**

```python
# From /unsloth/save.py __all__
print_quantization_methods      # Display quantization options
unsloth_save_model              # Main save function
save_to_gguf                    # GGUF export
patch_saving_functions          # Apply save methods to model
create_huggingface_repo         # Create HF repo
```

---

### 6. HOW SAVE METHODS ARE STRUCTURED

**Merge and Save Flow:**

```
Model with LoRA
    ↓
For each layer in model.layers:
    ├─ Extract: (W, A, B, scaling)
    ├─ Dequantize W if 4-bit
    ├─ Merge: W += A.T @ B.T * scaling
    └─ Save merged weights
    ↓
Save to disk/hub in chosen format
```

**Key layer weights merged:**
- Attention: q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj
- MLP: gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj
- LayerNorms: input_layernorm, post_attention_layernorm (NOT merged, just saved)

**Save directory structure for merged models:**
```
save_dir/
├── config.json                  # Model config (no quantization_config)
├── model.safetensors            # Merged weights in safetensors
├── tokenizer.model              # Tokenizer
├── tokenizer_config.json        # Tokenizer config
└── special_tokens_map.json      # Special tokens
```

**Save directory structure for LoRA-only:**
```
save_dir/
├── adapter_config.json          # LoRA config (r, alpha, target_modules)
├── adapter_model.safetensors    # LoRA A & B matrices
├── tokenizer.*                  # Tokenizer files
└── config.json                  # Model config
```

---

### 7. KEY FILES & THEIR PURPOSES

| File Path | Lines | Purpose |
|-----------|-------|---------|
| `unsloth/save.py` | 2,678 | Core save/export functionality; handles merging, GGUF, GGML |
| `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | ~700 | LoRA parameter extraction; quantization dequantize |
| `unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` | ~400 | Custom autograd for efficient LoRA forward/backward |
| `unsloth/models/llama.py` | ~2,000 | Llama model patching & LoRA integration |
| `unsloth/models/_utils.py` | ~1,800 | Shared utilities; LoRA patching into PEFT |
| `unsloth/models/loader.py` | ~1,000 | FastLanguageModel & FastModel loaders |
| `unsloth/__init__.py` | 270 | Package initialization; version management |
| `unsloth/models/__init__.py` | 29 | Model class exports |

---

### 8. CRITICAL INSIGHTS FOR LoRA ADAPTER WEIGHTS

**Storage pattern:**
- LoRA adapters stored in PEFT layers with dictionaries keyed by adapter name
- Default adapter: `"default"` key
- Multiple adapters supported simultaneously

**Access pattern to save LoRA weights:**
```python
for name, module in model.named_modules():
    if hasattr(module, 'lora_A'):  # Has LoRA
        for adapter_name, lora_a in module.lora_A.items():
            A = lora_a.weight  # Shape: (rank, in_features)
            B = module.lora_B[adapter_name].weight  # Shape: (out_features, rank)
            s = module.scaling[adapter_name]  # Float: scaling factor
            # ... save A, B, s ...
```

**Quantization state (4-bit models):**
- Stored in base weight: `W.quant_state` (from bitsandbytes)
- Needed to properly dequantize before merging
- Handled by `fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)` in kernels/utils.py

---

This completes the comprehensive exploration. The codebase is well-organized with clear separation between model loading (models/), optimization kernels (kernels/), and save/export functionality (save.py).

# Copyright 2023-present Daniel Han-Chen & the Unsloth team. All rights reserved.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.

import warnings, importlib, sys
from packaging.version import Version
import os, re, subprocess, inspect, functools
import numpy as np

# Fix some issues before importing other packages
from .import_fixes import fix_message_factory_issue
fix_message_factory_issue(); del fix_message_factory_issue;

# Check if modules that need patching are already imported
critical_modules = ['trl', 'transformers', 'peft']
already_imported = [mod for mod in critical_modules if mod in sys.modules]

# This check is critical because Unsloth optimizes these libraries by modifying
# their code at import time. If they're imported first, the original (slower,
# more memory-intensive) implementations will be used instead of Unsloth's
# optimized versions, potentially causing OOM errors or slower training.

if already_imported:
    # stacklevel=2 makes warning point to user's import line rather than this library code,
    # showing them exactly where to fix the import order in their script
    warnings.warn(
        f"WARNING: Unsloth should be imported before {', '.join(already_imported)} "
        f"to ensure all optimizations are applied. Your code may run slower or encounter "
        f"memory issues without these optimizations.\n\n"
        f"Please restructure your imports with 'import unsloth' at the top of your file.",
        stacklevel = 2,
    )
pass

# Unsloth currently does not work on multi GPU setups - sadly we are a 2 brother team so
# enabling it will require much more work, so we have to prioritize. Please understand!
# We do have a beta version, which you can contact us about!
# Thank you for your understanding and we appreciate it immensely!

# Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/1266
os.environ["PROTOCOL_BUFFERS_PYTHON_IMPLEMENTATION"] = "python"

# [TODO] Check why some GPUs don't work
#    "pinned_use_cuda_host_register:True,"\
#    "pinned_num_register_threads:8"

# Log Unsloth is being used
os.environ["UNSLOTH_IS_PRESENT"] = "1"

# Try importing PyTorch and check version
try:
    import torch
except ModuleNotFoundError:
    raise ImportError(
        "Unsloth: Pytorch is not installed. Go to https://pytorch.org/.\n"\
        "We have some installation instructions on our Github page."
    )
except Exception as exception:
    raise exception
pass

@functools.cache
def is_hip():
    return bool(getattr(getattr(torch, "version", None), "hip", None))
pass

@functools.cache
def get_device_type():
    if hasattr(torch, "cuda") and torch.cuda.is_available():
        if is_hip():
            return "hip"
        return "cuda"
    elif hasattr(torch, "xpu") and torch.xpu.is_available():
        return "xpu"
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth currently only works on NVIDIA GPUs and Intel GPUs.")
pass
DEVICE_TYPE : str = get_device_type()

@functools.cache
def get_device_count():
    if DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip"):
        return torch.cuda.device_count()
    elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
        return torch.xpu.device_count()
    else:
        return 1
pass

DEVICE_COUNT : int = get_device_count()

# Reduce VRAM usage by reducing fragmentation
# And optimize pinning of memory
# TODO(billishyahao): need to add hip related optimization...
if (DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip")) and (os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY", "0")=="0"):
    os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"] = \
        "expandable_segments:True,"\
        "roundup_power2_divisions:[32:256,64:128,256:64,>:32]"
elif (DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip")) and (os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY", "0")=="1") and \
    ("expandable_segments:True" in os.environ.get("PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF", "")):
    warnings.warn(
        "Unsloth: `UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY` is on, but requires `expandable_segments` to be off.\n"\
        "We will remove `expandable_segments`.",
        stacklevel = 2,
    )
    os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"] = re.sub(
        r"expandable\_segments\:True\,?",
        "",
        os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"],
    )
pass
# We support Pytorch 2
# Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/38
torch_version = str(re.match(r"[0-9\.]{3,}", str(torch.__version__)).group(0)).split(".")
major_torch, minor_torch = torch_version[0], torch_version[1]
major_torch, minor_torch = int(major_torch), int(minor_torch)
if (major_torch < 2):
    raise ImportError("Unsloth only supports Pytorch 2 for now. Please update your Pytorch to 2.1.\n"\
                      "We have some installation instructions on our Github page.")
elif (major_torch == 2) and (minor_torch < 2):
    # Disable expandable_segments
    del os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"]
pass

# CCE fails on Torch 2.8 and above
# OutOfResources: out of resource: shared memory, Required: 98304, Hardware limit: 65536. Reducing block sizes or `num_stages`
if (major_torch >= 2 and minor_torch >= 8) or (major_torch > 2):
    os.environ["UNSLOTH_ENABLE_CCE"] = "0"
pass

# Fix other issues
import importlib.util
from pathlib import Path
from importlib.metadata import version as importlib_version
from .import_fixes import fix_xformers_performance_issue
fix_xformers_performance_issue(); del fix_xformers_performance_issue;
from .import_fixes import fix_vllm_aimv2_issue
fix_vllm_aimv2_issue(); del fix_vllm_aimv2_issue;
from .import_fixes import ignore_logger_messages
ignore_logger_messages(); del ignore_logger_messages;

# Torch 2.4 has including_emulation
if DEVICE_TYPE == "cuda":
    major_version, minor_version = torch.cuda.get_device_capability()
    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = (major_version >= 8)

    old_is_bf16_supported = torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported
    if "including_emulation" in str(inspect.signature(old_is_bf16_supported)):
        def is_bf16_supported(including_emulation = False):
            return old_is_bf16_supported(including_emulation)
        torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported = is_bf16_supported
    else:
        def is_bf16_supported(): return SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16
        torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported = is_bf16_supported
    pass
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "hip":
    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported()
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
    # torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported() does not have including_emulation
    # set SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 as torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported()
    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported()
pass

# For Gradio HF Spaces?
# if "SPACE_AUTHOR_NAME" not in os.environ and "SPACE_REPO_NAME" not in os.environ:
import triton
if DEVICE_TYPE == "cuda":
    libcuda_dirs = lambda: None
    if Version(triton.__version__) >= Version("3.0.0"):
        try: from triton.backends.nvidia.driver import libcuda_dirs
        except: pass
    else: from triton.common.build import libcuda_dirs

    # Try loading bitsandbytes and triton
    import bitsandbytes as bnb
    try:
        cdequantize_blockwise_fp32 = bnb.functional.lib.cdequantize_blockwise_fp32
        libcuda_dirs()
    except:
        warnings.warn(
            "Unsloth: Running `ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` to link CUDA."\
        )

        if os.path.exists("/usr/lib64-nvidia"):
            os.system("ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia")
        elif os.path.exists("/usr/local"):
            # Sometimes bitsandbytes cannot be linked properly in Runpod for example
            possible_cudas = subprocess.check_output(["ls", "-al", "/usr/local"]).decode("utf-8").split("\n")
            find_cuda = re.compile(r"[\s](cuda\-[\d\.]{2,})$")
            possible_cudas = [find_cuda.search(x) for x in possible_cudas]
            possible_cudas = [x.group(1) for x in possible_cudas if x is not None]

            # Try linking cuda folder, or everything in local
            if len(possible_cudas) == 0:
                os.system("ldconfig /usr/local/")
            else:
                find_number = re.compile(r"([\d\.]{2,})")
                latest_cuda = np.argsort([float(find_number.search(x).group(1)) for x in possible_cudas])[::-1][0]
                latest_cuda = possible_cudas[latest_cuda]
                os.system(f"ldconfig /usr/local/{latest_cuda}")
        pass

        importlib.reload(bnb)
        importlib.reload(triton)
        try:
            libcuda_dirs = lambda: None
            if Version(triton.__version__) >= Version("3.0.0"):
                try: from triton.backends.nvidia.driver import libcuda_dirs
                except: pass
            else: from triton.common.build import libcuda_dirs
            cdequantize_blockwise_fp32 = bnb.functional.lib.cdequantize_blockwise_fp32
            libcuda_dirs()
        except:
            warnings.warn(
                "Unsloth: CUDA is not linked properly.\n"\
                "Try running `python -m bitsandbytes` then `python -m xformers.info`\n"\
                "We tried running `ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` ourselves, but it didn't work.\n"\
                "You need to run in your terminal `sudo ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` yourself, then import Unsloth.\n"\
                "Also try `sudo ldconfig /usr/local/cuda-xx.x` - find the latest cuda version.\n"\
                "Unsloth will still run for now, but maybe it might crash - let's hope it works!"
            )
    pass
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "hip":
    # NO-OP for rocm device
    pass
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
    # currently intel xpu will not support bnb, will add support in the future
    # TODO: check triton for intel installed properly.
    pass

# Check for unsloth_zoo
try:
    unsloth_zoo_version = importlib_version("unsloth_zoo")
    if Version(unsloth_zoo_version) < Version("2025.9.6"):
        print(
            "Unsloth: Please update Unsloth and Unsloth-Zoo to the latest version!\n"\
            "Do this via `pip install --upgrade --force-reinstall --no-cache-dir --no-deps unsloth unsloth_zoo`"
        )
        # if os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_DISABLE_AUTO_UPDATES", "0") == "0":
        #     try:
        #         os.system("pip install --upgrade --no-cache-dir --no-deps unsloth_zoo")
        #     except:
        #         try:
        #             os.system("pip install --upgrade --no-cache-dir --no-deps --user unsloth_zoo")
        #         except:
        #             raise ImportError("Unsloth: Please update unsloth_zoo via `pip install --upgrade --no-cache-dir --no-deps unsloth_zoo`")
    import unsloth_zoo
except:
    raise ImportError("Unsloth: Please install unsloth_zoo via `pip install unsloth_zoo`")
pass

from .models import *
from .models import __version__
from .save import *
from .chat_templates import *
from .tokenizer_utils import *
from .trainer import *

# Patch TRL trainers for backwards compatibility
_patch_trl_trainer()

from unsloth_zoo.utils import Version
from unsloth_zoo.hf_utils import dtype_from_config, HAS_TORCH_DTYPE
from bitsandbytes.nn import Linear4bit as Bnb_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear4bit as Peft_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear as Peft_Linear
from typing import Optional, Callable, Union, List
import sys
import requests
import torch
import os
import shutil
import pickle
import gc
from transformers.models.llama.modeling_llama import logger
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias
import subprocess
import psutil
import re
from transformers.models.llama.modeling_llama import logger
from .tokenizer_utils import fix_sentencepiece_gguf
from huggingface_hub import HfApi
try:
    from huggingface_hub import get_token
except:
    try:
        from huggingface_hub.utils import get_token
    except:
        # For older versions of huggingface_hub
        from huggingface_hub.utils._token import get_token
    pass
pass
from pathlib import Path

__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]

# llama.cpp specific targets - all takes 90s. Below takes 60s
LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS = ["llama-quantize", "llama-export-lora", "llama-cli",]

# Check environments
keynames = "\n" + "\n".join(os.environ.keys())
IS_COLAB_ENVIRONMENT  = "\nCOLAB_"  in keynames
IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT = "\nKAGGLE_" in keynames
KAGGLE_TMP = "/tmp"
del keynames

# Weights
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
    "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj",
)
LLAMA_LAYERNORMS = (
    "input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm",
    "pre_feedforward_layernorm", "post_feedforward_layernorm",
    "self_attn.q_norm", "self_attn.k_norm",
)

# https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/master/examples/quantize/quantize.cpp#L19
# From https://mlabonne.github.io/blog/posts/Quantize_Llama_2_models_using_ggml.html
ALLOWED_QUANTS = \
{
    "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
    "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
    "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
    "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
    "bf16"    : "Bfloat16 - Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
    "f16"     : "Float16  - Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
    "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
    "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
    "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
    "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
    "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
    "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
    "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
    "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
    "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
    "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
    "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
    "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
    "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
    "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
    "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
    "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
    # "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization", # Not supported sadly
    # "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
    # "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
    "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
}

def print_quantization_methods():
    for key, value in ALLOWED_QUANTS.items():
        print(f'"{key}"  ==> {value}')
    pass
pass


def check_if_sentencepiece_model(model, temporary_location = "_unsloth_sentencepiece_temp"):
    if not hasattr(model, "_saved_temp_tokenizer"): return False

    temp_tokenizer = model._saved_temp_tokenizer
    sentencepiece_model = False

def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
pass

def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
