def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

torch_float16  = torch.float16
torch_bfloat16 = torch.bfloat16

def QUANT_STATE(W): return getattr(W, "quant_state", None)

def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
    )
pass


def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
pass


def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
    """
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
    return X
pass


# INTEL GPU Specific Logic
if DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu" and HAS_XPU_STREAM:
    @torch.inference_mode
    def fast_dequantize(W, quant_state = None, out = None, use_global_buffer = False):
        # TODO: After adding XPU BNB support, check this function
        if quant_state is None: return W
        if type(quant_state) is not list:
            # New quant_state as a class
            # https://github.com/TimDettmers/bitsandbytes/pull/763/files
            absmax     = quant_state.absmax
            shape      = quant_state.shape

def unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    del arguments["self"]
    unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def unsloth_push_to_hub_merged(
    self,
    repo_id              : str,
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    token                : Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str, None] = "5GB",
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.push_to_hub(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]          = self
    arguments["save_directory"] = repo_id

def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion     : str = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
):
    """
        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.

        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["quantization_method"]
    del arguments["first_conversion"]

    # Fix tokenizer adding an extra BOS token at the front
    fix_bos_token, old_chat_template = fix_tokenizer_bos_token(tokenizer)

    # Non blocking install GGUF first
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):

        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()

input.content:
# Plan: Add `export_to_onnx` to unsloth

## Context

Unsloth has multiple save/export methods (GGUF, GGML, merged, TorchAO) but no ONNX export.
The goal is to add `export_to_onnx` that exports each LoRA adapter's A and B weight matrices
as ONNX computation graphs (the delta: `x @ A.T @ B.T * scaling`), saving metadata (rank,
alpha, target_modules) alongside. The method must be exposed as both a standalone function
and a bound method `model.save_pretrained_onnx(...)` alongside all existing save methods.

---

## Critical File

**Only one file needs modification:**

- `unsloth/save.py` — 2,679 lines; contains all save/export functions and `patch_saving_functions`

**Read-only references (no changes):**

- `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` — defines `get_lora_parameters(proj)` (line 190); provides the
  pattern for accessing `module.lora_A[adapter].weight`, `module.lora_B[adapter].weight`,
  `module.scaling[adapter]`
- `unsloth/kernels/__init__.py` — re-exports `get_lora_parameters`, `get_lora_parameters_bias`
  from `fast_lora.py` (which itself imports from `utils.py`)

---

## Existing Patterns to Follow

| Pattern | Where in save.py |
|---|---|
| `__all__` list of public exports | Lines 48–54 |
| Standalone function decorated `@torch.inference_mode` | Line 204 (`unsloth_save_model`) |
| Bound method wrapper calling standalone function | Lines 1321–1361 (`unsloth_save_pretrained_merged`) |
| Attaching bound method in `patch_saving_functions` | Lines 2657–2668 |

Key insight from `patch_saving_functions` (line 2569): non-vision models get all methods
attached inside `if not vision: if hasattr(model, "config"):` block (lines 2658–2668).

---

## Step-by-Step Changes to `unsloth/save.py`

### Change 1 — Add `"export_to_onnx"` to `__all__` (line 48)

```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
    "export_to_onnx",          # ← ADD THIS LINE
]
```

---

### Change 2 — Add `export_to_onnx` and `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx` functions

Insert **immediately before** `def patch_saving_functions` (currently line 2569). Add:

```python
@torch.inference_mode()
def export_to_onnx(
    model,
    output_dir    : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version : int = 17,
):
    """
    Export LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) to ONNX format.

    For each LoRA-adapted module, saves a .onnx file representing the
    delta computation:  output = (x @ lora_A.T @ lora_B.T) * scaling

    Also saves lora_metadata.json with rank, alpha, target_modules,
    opset_version, and a list of exported layer filenames.

    Args:
        model:          A PeftModel with LoRA adapters
                        (e.g. from FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model)
        output_dir:     Directory to save .onnx files and metadata JSON
        opset_version:  ONNX opset version (default: 17)

    Returns:
        output_dir (str)
    """
    try:
        import onnx
    except ImportError:
        raise ImportError(
            "Unsloth: Please install onnx via `pip install onnx` to use export_to_onnx."
        )

    if not hasattr(model, "peft_config"):
        raise ValueError(
            "Unsloth: export_to_onnx requires a PEFT model with LoRA adapters. "
            "Use FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(...) first."
        )

    output_dir = str(output_dir)
    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

    # Extract LoRA configuration metadata
    adapter_name   = list(model.peft_config.keys())[0]   # typically "default"
    peft_config    = model.peft_config[adapter_name]
    rank           = peft_config.r
    alpha          = peft_config.lora_alpha
    target_modules = list(peft_config.target_modules) if peft_config.target_modules else []

    # Minimal nn.Module wrapping the LoRA delta computation
    class _LoraAdapterModule(torch.nn.Module):
        def __init__(self, A, B, scaling):
            super().__init__()
            self.lora_A  = torch.nn.Parameter(A.detach().float().cpu(), requires_grad=False)
            self.lora_B  = torch.nn.Parameter(B.detach().float().cpu(), requires_grad=False)
            self.scaling = float(scaling)

        def forward(self, x):
            # x: (..., in_features)  ->  output: (..., out_features)
            return (x @ self.lora_A.t() @ self.lora_B.t()) * self.scaling

    exported_layers = []

    for module_name, module in model.named_modules():
        if not (hasattr(module, "lora_A") and hasattr(module, "lora_B")):
            continue
        if getattr(module, "disable_adapters", False) or getattr(module, "merged", False):
            continue

        # Resolve active adapter name
        active = getattr(module, "active_adapters", None)
        if active is None:
            active = getattr(module, "active_adapter", ("default",))
        if isinstance(active, str):
            active = (active,)
        active = active[0]

        if active not in module.lora_A:
            continue

        A       = module.lora_A[active].weight   # (rank, in_features)
        B       = module.lora_B[active].weight   # (out_features, rank)
        scaling = module.scaling[active]          # float scalar

        in_features  = A.shape[1]
        out_features = B.shape[0]

        lora_module = _LoraAdapterModule(A, B, scaling)
        lora_module.eval()

        # Dummy input: (1, in_features)
        dummy_input = torch.zeros(1, in_features, dtype=torch.float32)

        # Sanitize module_name → filename  (dots/slashes → underscores)
        safe_name = module_name.replace(".", "_").replace("/", "_")
        onnx_path = os.path.join(output_dir, f"{safe_name}.onnx")

        torch.onnx.export(
            lora_module,
            dummy_input,
            onnx_path,
            opset_version       = opset_version,
            input_names         = ["input"],
            output_names        = ["lora_delta"],
            dynamic_axes        = {
                "input":      {0: "batch_size"},
                "lora_delta": {0: "batch_size"},
            },
            do_constant_folding = True,
        )

        # Validate the exported model
        onnx.checker.check_model(onnx_path)

        exported_layers.append({
            "module_name": module_name,
            "file":        f"{safe_name}.onnx",
            "in_features": in_features,
            "out_features": out_features,
        })
    pass

    if not exported_layers:
        raise ValueError(
            "Unsloth: No active LoRA adapter layers found to export. "
            "Ensure the model has non-merged LoRA adapters."
        )

    # Write metadata JSON
    import json
    metadata = {
        "adapter_name":   adapter_name,
        "rank":           rank,
        "alpha":          alpha,
        "target_modules": target_modules,
        "opset_version":  opset_version,
        "layers":         exported_layers,
    }
    metadata_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "lora_metadata.json")
    with open(metadata_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(metadata, f, indent=2)

    print(f"Unsloth: Exported {len(exported_layers)} LoRA adapter(s) to '{output_dir}'")
    print(f"Unsloth: Metadata saved to '{metadata_path}'")
    return output_dir
pass


def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx(
    self,
    output_dir    : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version : int = 17,
):
    """
    Export LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) to ONNX format.

    Each LoRA-adapted module is saved as a separate .onnx file representing
    the delta computation:  output = (x @ lora_A.T @ lora_B.T) * scaling

    Also saves lora_metadata.json with rank, alpha, target_modules, and
    a list of exported layer filenames.

    Args:
        output_dir:     Directory to write .onnx files and lora_metadata.json
        opset_version:  ONNX opset version (default: 17)
    """
    export_to_onnx(self, output_dir=output_dir, opset_version=opset_version)
pass
```

---

### Change 3 — Attach `save_pretrained_onnx` in `patch_saving_functions`

Inside the block `if not vision: if hasattr(model, "config"):` (currently lines 2658–2668),
add one line after `model.save_pretrained_torchao`:

```python
# existing lines
model.push_to_hub_merged       = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
model.save_pretrained_merged   = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf         = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf     = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
model.save_pretrained_torchao  = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,               model)
model.push_to_hub_ggml         = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
model.save_pretrained_ggml     = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
model.save_pretrained_onnx     = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx,                  model)  # ← ADD
```

Also add to the **vision** branch (currently lines 2670–2675) for consistency:

```python
model.save_pretrained_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx, model)  # ← ADD
```

---

## Output Directory Structure

After `export_to_onnx("./onnx_adapters")`:

```
onnx_adapters/
├── lora_metadata.json                              # rank, alpha, target_modules, layer list
├── base_model_model_model_layers_0_self_attn_q_proj.onnx
```

# Plan: Add `export_to_onnx` to unsloth

## Context

Unsloth has multiple save/export methods (GGUF, GGML, merged, TorchAO) but no ONNX export.
The goal is to add `export_to_onnx` that exports each LoRA adapter's A and B weight matrices
as ONNX computation graphs (the delta: `x @ A.T @ B.T * scaling`), saving metadata (rank,
alpha, target_modules) alongside. The method must be exposed as both a standalone function
and a bound method `model.save_pretrained_onnx(...)` alongside all existing save methods.

---

## Critical File

**Only one file needs modification:**

- `unsloth/save.py` — 2,679 lines; contains all save/export functions and `patch_saving_functions`

**Read-only references (no changes):**

- `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` — defines `get_lora_parameters(proj)` (line 190); provides the
  pattern for accessing `module.lora_A[adapter].weight`, `module.lora_B[adapter].weight`,
  `module.scaling[adapter]`
- `unsloth/kernels/__init__.py` — re-exports `get_lora_parameters`, `get_lora_parameters_bias`
  from `fast_lora.py` (which itself imports from `utils.py`)

---

## Existing Patterns to Follow

| Pattern | Where in save.py |
|---|---|
| `__all__` list of public exports | Lines 48–54 |
| Standalone function decorated `@torch.inference_mode` | Line 204 (`unsloth_save_model`) |
| Bound method wrapper calling standalone function | Lines 1321–1361 (`unsloth_save_pretrained_merged`) |
| Attaching bound method in `patch_saving_functions` | Lines 2657–2668 |

Key insight from `patch_saving_functions` (line 2569): non-vision models get all methods
attached inside `if not vision: if hasattr(model, "config"):` block (lines 2658–2668).

---

## Step-by-Step Changes to `unsloth/save.py`

### Change 1 — Add `"export_to_onnx"` to `__all__` (line 48)

```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
    "export_to_onnx",          # ← ADD THIS LINE
]
```

---

### Change 2 — Add `export_to_onnx` and `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx` functions

Insert **immediately before** `def patch_saving_functions` (currently line 2569). Add:

```python
@torch.inference_mode()
def export_to_onnx(
    model,
    output_dir    : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version : int = 17,
):
    """
    Export LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) to ONNX format.

    For each LoRA-adapted module, saves a .onnx file representing the
    delta computation:  output = (x @ lora_A.T @ lora_B.T) * scaling

    Also saves lora_metadata.json with rank, alpha, target_modules,
    opset_version, and a list of exported layer filenames.

    Args:
        model:          A PeftModel with LoRA adapters
                        (e.g. from FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model)
        output_dir:     Directory to save .onnx files and metadata JSON
        opset_version:  ONNX opset version (default: 17)

    Returns:
        output_dir (str)
    """
    try:
        import onnx
    except ImportError:
        raise ImportError(
            "Unsloth: Please install onnx via `pip install onnx` to use export_to_onnx."
        )

    if not hasattr(model, "peft_config"):
        raise ValueError(
            "Unsloth: export_to_onnx requires a PEFT model with LoRA adapters. "
            "Use FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(...) first."
        )

    output_dir = str(output_dir)
    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

    # Extract LoRA configuration metadata
    adapter_name   = list(model.peft_config.keys())[0]   # typically "default"
    peft_config    = model.peft_config[adapter_name]
    rank           = peft_config.r
    alpha          = peft_config.lora_alpha
    target_modules = list(peft_config.target_modules) if peft_config.target_modules else []

    # Minimal nn.Module wrapping the LoRA delta computation
    class _LoraAdapterModule(torch.nn.Module):
        def __init__(self, A, B, scaling):
            super().__init__()
            self.lora_A  = torch.nn.Parameter(A.detach().float().cpu(), requires_grad=False)
            self.lora_B  = torch.nn.Parameter(B.detach().float().cpu(), requires_grad=False)
            self.scaling = float(scaling)

        def forward(self, x):
            # x: (..., in_features)  ->  output: (..., out_features)
            return (x @ self.lora_A.t() @ self.lora_B.t()) * self.scaling

    exported_layers = []

    for module_name, module in model.named_modules():
        if not (hasattr(module, "lora_A") and hasattr(module, "lora_B")):
            continue
        if getattr(module, "disable_adapters", False) or getattr(module, "merged", False):
            continue

        # Resolve active adapter name
        active = getattr(module, "active_adapters", None)
        if active is None:
            active = getattr(module, "active_adapter", ("default",))
        if isinstance(active, str):
            active = (active,)
        active = active[0]

        if active not in module.lora_A:
            continue

        A       = module.lora_A[active].weight   # (rank, in_features)
        B       = module.lora_B[active].weight   # (out_features, rank)
        scaling = module.scaling[active]          # float scalar

        in_features  = A.shape[1]
        out_features = B.shape[0]

        lora_module = _LoraAdapterModule(A, B, scaling)
        lora_module.eval()

        # Dummy input: (1, in_features)
        dummy_input = torch.zeros(1, in_features, dtype=torch.float32)

        # Sanitize module_name → filename  (dots/slashes → underscores)
        safe_name = module_name.replace(".", "_").replace("/", "_")
        onnx_path = os.path.join(output_dir, f"{safe_name}.onnx")

        torch.onnx.export(
            lora_module,
            dummy_input,
            onnx_path,
            opset_version       = opset_version,
            input_names         = ["input"],
            output_names        = ["lora_delta"],
            dynamic_axes        = {
                "input":      {0: "batch_size"},
                "lora_delta": {0: "batch_size"},
            },
            do_constant_folding = True,
        )

        # Validate the exported model
        onnx.checker.check_model(onnx_path)

        exported_layers.append({
            "module_name": module_name,
            "file":        f"{safe_name}.onnx",
            "in_features": in_features,
            "out_features": out_features,
        })
    pass

    if not exported_layers:
        raise ValueError(
            "Unsloth: No active LoRA adapter layers found to export. "
            "Ensure the model has non-merged LoRA adapters."
        )

    # Write metadata JSON
    import json
    metadata = {
        "adapter_name":   adapter_name,
        "rank":           rank,
        "alpha":          alpha,
        "target_modules": target_modules,
        "opset_version":  opset_version,
        "layers":         exported_layers,
    }
    metadata_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "lora_metadata.json")
    with open(metadata_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(metadata, f, indent=2)

    print(f"Unsloth: Exported {len(exported_layers)} LoRA adapter(s) to '{output_dir}'")
    print(f"Unsloth: Metadata saved to '{metadata_path}'")
    return output_dir
pass


def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx(
    self,
    output_dir    : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version : int = 17,
):
    """
    Export LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) to ONNX format.

    Each LoRA-adapted module is saved as a separate .onnx file representing
    the delta computation:  output = (x @ lora_A.T @ lora_B.T) * scaling

    Also saves lora_metadata.json with rank, alpha, target_modules, and
    a list of exported layer filenames.

    Args:
        output_dir:     Directory to write .onnx files and lora_metadata.json
        opset_version:  ONNX opset version (default: 17)
    """
    export_to_onnx(self, output_dir=output_dir, opset_version=opset_version)
pass
```

---

### Change 3 — Attach `save_pretrained_onnx` in `patch_saving_functions`

Inside the block `if not vision: if hasattr(model, "config"):` (currently lines 2658–2668),
add one line after `model.save_pretrained_torchao`:

```python
model.push_to_hub_ggml         = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)

```

Also add to the **vision** branch (currently lines 2670–2675) for consistency:

```
├── base_model_model_model_layers_0_self_attn_o_proj.onnx
├── base_model_model_model_layers_0_mlp_gate_proj.onnx
├── ...
└── (one file per LoRA-enabled module across all layers)
```

`lora_metadata.json` example:

```json
  "adapter_name": "default",
  "rank": 16,
  "alpha": 16,
  "target_modules": ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"],
  "opset_version": 17,
  "layers": [
    {"module_name": "base_model.model.model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj",
     "file": "base_model_model_model_layers_0_self_attn_q_proj.onnx",

  ]
}
```

```json
    {"module_name": "base_model.model.model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj",
     "file": "base_model_model_model_layers_0_self_attn_q_proj.onnx",
     "in_features": 4096, "out_features": 4096},
    ...
  ]
}
```

---

## Public API After Changes

```python
from unsloth import export_to_onnx

# Standalone
export_to_onnx(model, "./onnx_out", opset_version=17)

# Bound method (after FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained + get_peft_model)
model.save_pretrained_onnx("./onnx_out")
model.save_pretrained_onnx("./onnx_out", opset_version=18)
```

## Verification

1. **Import check:** `from unsloth import export_to_onnx` should succeed
2. **Method presence:** after `patch_saving_functions`, `hasattr(model, "save_pretrained_onnx")` is `True`
3. **Functional test (no GPU needed, mock):**

Here's the complete plan. All changes are **isolated to a single file: `unsloth/save.py`**.

---

## What I found

- **No ONNX code exists** anywhere in the codebase — this is a clean addition
- All save/export methods live in `unsloth/save.py` and are attached to model instances via `patch_saving_functions()` using `types.MethodType`
- LoRA A/B matrices are accessed via `module.lora_A[adapter].weight` and `module.lora_B[adapter].weight`; config metadata (rank, alpha, target_modules) via `model.peft_config["default"]`
- The existing pattern: standalone function → thin bound-method wrapper → attached in `patch_saving_functions`

---

## Three precise edits to `unsloth/save.py`

| # | Location | What |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `__all__` (line 48) | Add `"export_to_onnx"` so it's importable from `unsloth` |
| 2 | Before `patch_saving_functions` (~line 2569) | Add `export_to_onnx()` (standalone, `@torch.inference_mode`) and `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx()` (bound-method wrapper) |
| 3 | Inside `patch_saving_functions` (lines 2661 & 2675) | Attach `model.save_pretrained_onnx` for both non-vision and vision branches |

---

## What `export_to_onnx` does

1. Validates the model has `peft_config` (is a PEFT model)
2. Reads `rank`, `alpha`, `target_modules` from `model.peft_config`
3. Iterates `model.named_modules()`, skipping layers without `lora_A`/`lora_B` or with merged/disabled adapters
4. For each LoRA layer, wraps A + B into a tiny `nn.Module` computing `(x @ A.T @ B.T) * scaling`
5. Exports each to a `.onnx` file via `torch.onnx.export` with the given `opset_version` (default 17) and validates with `onnx.checker.check_model`
6. Writes `lora_metadata.json` with rank, alpha, target_modules, opset_version, and per-layer file list
7. `onnx` is imported lazily inside the function (soft dependency — not required at unsloth import time)

tool_input.plan:
# Plan: Add `export_to_onnx` to unsloth

## Context

Unsloth has multiple save/export methods (GGUF, GGML, merged, TorchAO) but no ONNX export.
The goal is to add `export_to_onnx` that exports each LoRA adapter's A and B weight matrices
as ONNX computation graphs (the delta: `x @ A.T @ B.T * scaling`), saving metadata (rank,
alpha, target_modules) alongside. The method must be exposed as both a standalone function
and a bound method `model.save_pretrained_onnx(...)` alongside all existing save methods.

---

## Critical File

**Only one file needs modification:**

- `unsloth/save.py` — 2,679 lines; contains all save/export functions and `patch_saving_functions`

**Read-only references (no changes):**

- `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` — defines `get_lora_parameters(proj)` (line 190); provides the
  pattern for accessing `module.lora_A[adapter].weight`, `module.lora_B[adapter].weight`,
  `module.scaling[adapter]`
- `unsloth/kernels/__init__.py` — re-exports `get_lora_parameters`, `get_lora_parameters_bias`
  from `fast_lora.py` (which itself imports from `utils.py`)

---

## Existing Patterns to Follow

| Pattern | Where in save.py |
|---|---|
| `__all__` list of public exports | Lines 48–54 |
| Standalone function decorated `@torch.inference_mode` | Line 204 (`unsloth_save_model`) |
| Bound method wrapper calling standalone function | Lines 1321–1361 (`unsloth_save_pretrained_merged`) |
| Attaching bound method in `patch_saving_functions` | Lines 2657–2668 |

Key insight from `patch_saving_functions` (line 2569): non-vision models get all methods
attached inside `if not vision: if hasattr(model, "config"):` block (lines 2658–2668).

---

## Step-by-Step Changes to `unsloth/save.py`

### Change 1 — Add `"export_to_onnx"` to `__all__` (line 48)

```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
    "export_to_onnx",          # ← ADD THIS LINE
]
```

---

### Change 2 — Add `export_to_onnx` and `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx` functions

Insert **immediately before** `def patch_saving_functions` (currently line 2569). Add:

```python
@torch.inference_mode()
def export_to_onnx(
    model,
    output_dir    : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version : int = 17,
):
    """
    Export LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) to ONNX format.

    For each LoRA-adapted module, saves a .onnx file representing the
    delta computation:  output = (x @ lora_A.T @ lora_B.T) * scaling

    Also saves lora_metadata.json with rank, alpha, target_modules,
    opset_version, and a list of exported layer filenames.

    Args:
        model:          A PeftModel with LoRA adapters
                        (e.g. from FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model)
        output_dir:     Directory to save .onnx files and metadata JSON
        opset_version:  ONNX opset version (default: 17)

    Returns:
        output_dir (str)
    """
    try:
        import onnx
    except ImportError:
        raise ImportError(
            "Unsloth: Please install onnx via `pip install onnx` to use export_to_onnx."
        )

    if not hasattr(model, "peft_config"):
        raise ValueError(
            "Unsloth: export_to_onnx requires a PEFT model with LoRA adapters. "
            "Use FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(...) first."
        )

    output_dir = str(output_dir)
    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

    # Extract LoRA configuration metadata
    adapter_name   = list(model.peft_config.keys())[0]   # typically "default"
    peft_config    = model.peft_config[adapter_name]
    rank           = peft_config.r
    alpha          = peft_config.lora_alpha
    target_modules = list(peft_config.target_modules) if peft_config.target_modules else []

    # Minimal nn.Module wrapping the LoRA delta computation
    class _LoraAdapterModule(torch.nn.Module):
        def __init__(self, A, B, scaling):
            super().__init__()
            self.lora_A  = torch.nn.Parameter(A.detach().float().cpu(), requires_grad=False)
            self.lora_B  = torch.nn.Parameter(B.detach().float().cpu(), requires_grad=False)
            self.scaling = float(scaling)

        def forward(self, x):
            # x: (..., in_features)  ->  output: (..., out_features)
            return (x @ self.lora_A.t() @ self.lora_B.t()) * self.scaling

    exported_layers = []

    for module_name, module in model.named_modules():
        if not (hasattr(module, "lora_A") and hasattr(module, "lora_B")):
            continue
        if getattr(module, "disable_adapters", False) or getattr(module, "merged", False):
            continue

        # Resolve active adapter name
        active = getattr(module, "active_adapters", None)
        if active is None:
            active = getattr(module, "active_adapter", ("default",))
        if isinstance(active, str):
            active = (active,)
        active = active[0]

        if active not in module.lora_A:
            continue

        A       = module.lora_A[active].weight   # (rank, in_features)
        B       = module.lora_B[active].weight   # (out_features, rank)
        scaling = module.scaling[active]          # float scalar

        in_features  = A.shape[1]
        out_features = B.shape[0]

        lora_module = _LoraAdapterModule(A, B, scaling)
        lora_module.eval()

        # Dummy input: (1, in_features)
        dummy_input = torch.zeros(1, in_features, dtype=torch.float32)

        # Sanitize module_name → filename  (dots/slashes → underscores)
        safe_name = module_name.replace(".", "_").replace("/", "_")
        onnx_path = os.path.join(output_dir, f"{safe_name}.onnx")

        torch.onnx.export(
            lora_module,
            dummy_input,
            onnx_path,
            opset_version       = opset_version,
            input_names         = ["input"],
            output_names        = ["lora_delta"],
            dynamic_axes        = {
                "input":      {0: "batch_size"},
                "lora_delta": {0: "batch_size"},
            },
            do_constant_folding = True,
        )

        # Validate the exported model
        onnx.checker.check_model(onnx_path)

        exported_layers.append({
            "module_name": module_name,
            "file":        f"{safe_name}.onnx",
            "in_features": in_features,
            "out_features": out_features,
        })
    pass

    if not exported_layers:
        raise ValueError(
            "Unsloth: No active LoRA adapter layers found to export. "
            "Ensure the model has non-merged LoRA adapters."
        )

    # Write metadata JSON
    import json
    metadata = {
        "adapter_name":   adapter_name,
        "rank":           rank,
        "alpha":          alpha,
        "target_modules": target_modules,
        "opset_version":  opset_version,
        "layers":         exported_layers,
    }
    metadata_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "lora_metadata.json")
    with open(metadata_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(metadata, f, indent=2)

    print(f"Unsloth: Exported {len(exported_layers)} LoRA adapter(s) to '{output_dir}'")
    print(f"Unsloth: Metadata saved to '{metadata_path}'")
    return output_dir
pass


def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx(
    self,
    output_dir    : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version : int = 17,
):
    """
    Export LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) to ONNX format.

    Each LoRA-adapted module is saved as a separate .onnx file representing
    the delta computation:  output = (x @ lora_A.T @ lora_B.T) * scaling

    Also saves lora_metadata.json with rank, alpha, target_modules, and
    a list of exported layer filenames.

    Args:
        output_dir:     Directory to write .onnx files and lora_metadata.json
        opset_version:  ONNX opset version (default: 17)
    """
    export_to_onnx(self, output_dir=output_dir, opset_version=opset_version)
pass
```

---

### Change 3 — Attach `save_pretrained_onnx` in `patch_saving_functions`

Inside the block `if not vision: if hasattr(model, "config"):` (currently lines 2658–2668),
add one line after `model.save_pretrained_torchao`:

```python
# existing lines
model.push_to_hub_merged       = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
model.save_pretrained_merged   = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf         = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf     = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
model.save_pretrained_torchao  = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,               model)
model.push_to_hub_ggml         = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
model.save_pretrained_ggml     = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
model.save_pretrained_onnx     = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx,                  model)  # ← ADD
```

Also add to the **vision** branch (currently lines 2670–2675) for consistency:

```python
model.save_pretrained_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx, model)  # ← ADD
```

---

## Output Directory Structure

After `export_to_onnx("./onnx_adapters")`:

```
onnx_adapters/
├── lora_metadata.json                              # rank, alpha, target_modules, layer list
├── base_model_model_model_layers_0_self_attn_q_proj.onnx
├── base_model_model_model_layers_0_self_attn_k_proj.onnx
├── base_model_model_model_layers_0_self_attn_v_proj.onnx
├── base_model_model_model_layers_0_self_attn_o_proj.onnx
├── base_model_model_model_layers_0_mlp_gate_proj.onnx
├── ...
└── (one file per LoRA-enabled module across all layers)
```

`lora_metadata.json` example:
```json
{
  "adapter_name": "default",
  "rank": 16,
  "alpha": 16,
  "target_modules": ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"],
  "opset_version": 17,
  "layers": [
    {"module_name": "base_model.model.model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj",
     "file": "base_model_model_model_layers_0_self_attn_q_proj.onnx",
     "in_features": 4096, "out_features": 4096},
    ...
  ]
}
```

---

## Public API After Changes

```python
from unsloth import export_to_onnx

# Standalone
export_to_onnx(model, "./onnx_out", opset_version=17)

# Bound method (after FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained + get_peft_model)
model.save_pretrained_onnx("./onnx_out")
model.save_pretrained_onnx("./onnx_out", opset_version=18)
```

---

## Verification

1. **Import check:** `from unsloth import export_to_onnx` should succeed
2. **Method presence:** after `patch_saving_functions`, `hasattr(model, "save_pretrained_onnx")` is `True`
3. **Functional test (no GPU needed, mock):**
   ```python
   import torch, json, os
   from unittest.mock import MagicMock
   # Build minimal fake PEFT model with lora_A / lora_B
   # Call export_to_onnx(fake_model, tmp_dir)
   # Assert lora_metadata.json exists and contains correct rank/alpha
   # Assert .onnx files exist for each mocked module
   # Assert onnx.checker.check_model passes on each file
   ```
4. **Opset test:** call with `opset_version=15` and verify the ONNX file's ir_version changes
5. **Error path:** call on a non-PEFT model → `ValueError` raised correctly
6. **Error path:** call on model with all adapters merged → `ValueError` (no layers found)

def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

# Weights
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",

# https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/master/examples/quantize/quantize.cpp#L19
# From https://mlabonne.github.io/blog/posts/Quantize_Llama_2_models_using_ggml.html
ALLOWED_QUANTS = \
{
    "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
    "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
    "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
    "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
    "bf16"    : "Bfloat16 - Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
    "f16"     : "Float16  - Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
    "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
    "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
    "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
    "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
    "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
    "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
    "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
    "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
    "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
    "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
    "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
    "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
    "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
    "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
    "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
    "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
    # "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization", # Not supported sadly
